snippets.title: IQR outlier flags with pandas
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd

s = pd.Series([10, 12, 11, 13, 12, 100, 11, 12])
q1, q3 = s.quantile([0.25, 0.75])
iqr = q3 - q1
lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr
upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr
is_outlier = (s < lower) | (s > upper)
print("bounds:", lower, upper)
print(is_outlier.tolist())
snippets.explanation: Tukey's 1.5×IQR rule flags 100 as an outlier while quartiles stay stable because only one point is extreme.

snippets.title: Z-score detection
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

x = np.array([10.0, 11.0, 12.0, 10.5, 11.5, 200.0])
mu = x.mean()
sigma = x.std(ddof=1)
z = (x - mu) / sigma
is_outlier = np.abs(z) > 3
print("z:", np.round(z, 2))
print("outliers:", is_outlier)
snippets.explanation: The 200 pulls μ and σ upward, so z-scores for normal points shrink—illustrating why z-scores are not robust. Compare with IQR on the same array.

snippets.title: Clip (winsorize) using train-only bounds
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
train = pd.Series(rng.normal(50, 10, 500))
test = pd.Series(rng.normal(50, 10, 100))

lo, hi = train.quantile([0.01, 0.99])  # fit on train only
train_clipped = train.clip(lo, hi)
test_clipped = test.clip(lo, hi)
print("train max before/after:", train.max(), train_clipped.max())
snippets.explanation: Percentile bounds come from training data; the same lo/hi cap validation and test without peeking at their distributions.

snippets.title: Robust regression with Huber loss (scikit-learn)
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.linear_model import HuberRegressor

rng = np.random.default_rng(1)
X = rng.normal(size=(100, 1))
y = 3 * X.squeeze() + rng.normal(scale=0.5, size=100)
y[0] = 50  # one outlier in target

huber = HuberRegressor().fit(X, y)
print("Huber coef ~3:", round(huber.coef_[0], 2))
snippets.explanation: Huber loss behaves like MSE near zero error but like MAE for large residuals, so one bad y[0] does not destroy the slope estimate.
